originalTitle: France returns sacred 'talking drum' looted during colonial rule to Ivory Coast
title: A Special Talking Drum Comes Home to Africa!
content:
Hello, Friends! Guess What's Happening?

Hello everyone! Do you like exciting news? We have some very happy news from Africa today! Something very special has travelled all the way back to its home. It’s a very old and very important drum. This drum is now back in a country called Ivory Coast. Everyone there is super excited!

Meet the Amazing Talking Drum!

This is not just any drum, friends! It's called a 'talking drum'. Can you imagine a drum that talks? Well, it doesn't really speak words like you and I do. But it can make many different sounds! These sounds can be like secret messages. People in Africa have used talking drums for a very, very long time. They used them to tell stories, send news to people far away, and even talk to each other across long distances. How cool is that? Did you know? Drummers can change the sound of a talking drum by squeezing the ropes that hold its sides together! It makes the drum sound a bit like a human voice singing.

Where is Ivory Coast?

Ivory Coast is a beautiful country in Africa. It's on the west side of Africa, right next to the big ocean. The sun shines brightly there! People in Ivory Coast speak French and many other wonderful languages. They also grow a lot of cocoa beans, which are used to make yummy chocolate! The people of Ivory Coast are very, very happy to see their special drum come back. It's like finding a lost toy you loved very much, only much bigger and more important! Did you know? Ivory Coast is one of the biggest cocoa producers in the world! So, next time you enjoy a chocolate bar, remember the happy farmers in Ivory Coast.

Why Was the Drum Away From Home?

This talking drum was taken from Ivory Coast a long, long time ago. It was during a time called 'colonial rule'. What is colonial rule? Imagine if people from one big country came to another country and decided to be in charge. They would sometimes take special things from that country, like beautiful art or important tools. This drum was one of those special things. It was taken to a far-away country called France.

Where is France?

France is a country in Europe. It's very far from Africa, across a big ocean. France has famous places like the Eiffel Tower, which is a very tall and sparkly building! A long time ago, France was one of the countries that had 'colonial rule' over many parts of Africa, including Ivory Coast. So, the drum ended up in France. It has been kept in a museum there for many, many years, where people could look at it. Did you know? The Eiffel Tower in France is visited by millions of people every year! It's one of the most famous buildings on Earth.

Why is Getting it Back So Important?

When the drum was taken, the people of Ivory Coast felt very sad. This drum is more than just an old drum. It is a very big part of their history. It tells stories about their grand-grand-grandparents and their culture. Culture is all the special ways people live, like their music, their art, their stories, and their traditions. Getting the drum back helps them remember who they are and where they come from. It's like getting a piece of their heart back, a very important piece of their past. Did you know? Many cultures around the world use drums to celebrate, tell stories, and even for healing! Drums are very powerful instruments that connect people.

More Treasures Are Coming Home!

This special talking drum is not the only thing going back home to Africa! There are many other special things, like beautiful statues and masks, that were also taken a long, long time ago. Many African countries want these treasures back because they belong in their homes. In 2017, some good people decided it was time to start bringing these items home. It’s like a big "return home" party for lots and lots of African treasures! Did you know? Museums sometimes keep things that are very old or special so people can learn about the past. But it's also important for these special items to be in their home countries, close to the people they belong to.

A Happy Day for Everyone!

So, the talking drum is finally back in Ivory Coast. This is a very happy day for everyone! It shows that people can learn from the past and do the right thing. Countries are working together to make sure these special cultural items are returned to their rightful homes. It helps people feel proud of their history and culture. And it teaches us all about sharing, respect, and why it's important to keep our special things safe and at home. What a wonderful story, isn't it?
summary: France has returned a very old and important 'talking drum' to Ivory Coast, bringing joy and a piece of history back to its home country in Africa.
whyMatters: This is important because it helps people get their special history and culture back, showing that countries can work together to make things right and respect where items truly belong.

originalTitle: Ghana star to deny rape charges, UK court hears
title: Our Ghana Football Hero Partey Goes to Court!
content:
Hello, Young Friends from Africa!

Guess what? We have some important news to share today, especially for all of you who love football! You know how much we love our football heroes, right? They run, they kick, they score amazing goals, and they make our hearts sing with pride for Ghana!

Who is Partey?

We are talking about a very famous football player from our own country, Ghana! His name is Partey. Maybe you have seen him play on TV? He wears the Ghana jersey, which is red, yellow, and green, just like our flag! He also plays for a very big team in a country called the United Kingdom. Partey is a super strong player in the middle of the field, helping his team win many games. He is a true superstar!

A Special Place Called Court

Sometimes, when grown-ups have a big problem or they need to figure out something important, they go to a special place. This place is called a court. Imagine it like a very, very big meeting, but with special rules! In a court, people talk about what happened, and a kind judge helps everyone listen and try to understand the truth. It's all about making sure things are fair for everyone. **Did you know?** Courts are in almost every country in the world! They help make sure rules are followed and that people are treated fairly. It's like having a referee for big life problems!

Partey Needs to Talk About Something Important

Now, here's the news about Partey. He will be going to a court in the United Kingdom next month. This is because there is a serious matter that happened a few years ago, in December 2020. Someone said something about what happened then. And Partey says that's not exactly how it happened. He wants to go to court to explain his side of the story. This is a very serious thing, and it means people need to listen carefully to everyone involved. The court is where everyone gets a chance to speak and be heard.

Why Do People Go to Court?

Imagine if you and your friend had a disagreement about whose turn it was to play with a toy. Sometimes, you need a grown-up to help you figure it out, right? A court is like a super-grown-up helper for very big problems. It's a place where we try to find the truth, even when things are difficult. The court wants to make sure that everyone follows rules, and that if something wrong happened, it can be made right. It's about being fair to everyone. **Did you know?** In court, there are usually people called lawyers. They are like special helpers who know a lot about rules and help people tell their stories to the judge!

What Partey Will Do in Court

When Partey goes to court, he will have a chance to tell his story. He will get to say that what someone said about December 2020 is not true, or not completely true. This is called "denying" something. It means he is saying, "No, that's not how it was." It's very important for everyone to listen to what Partey says, and also to what other people say. The judge's job is to listen to all the stories, look at all the facts, and then try to understand what really happened. It takes time and careful listening.

Why Fairness is Super Important

When we play football, we have rules, right? No pushing, no tripping, play fair! Life also has rules. We should always be kind to each other, respectful, and make sure everyone feels safe. When something happens that makes people feel unsafe or unhappy, that's a big problem. Courts are there to help us remember these important rules and to make sure that everyone is treated fairly, no matter who they are – whether they are a famous footballer or any other person. It means that if someone says something happened, and the other person says it didn't, there's a place to talk about it and find out the truth. **Did you know?** Many countries have special laws to protect everyone, especially children and women, to make sure they are safe and treated with respect.

What Happens Next?

Partey will go to court soon. He will talk about what happened. It might take some time for the court to hear everything and make a decision. This is how the system works to make sure everything is done carefully and fairly. We hope that the court will help everyone understand the truth and that everything will be sorted out. We should always remember that everyone deserves to be treated with respect, and everyone has a right to tell their side of the story.

Our Football Heroes and Us

Our football heroes like Partey bring us so much joy and make us proud. They are like role models for many of you. This news reminds us that even grown-ups, even famous ones, sometimes have big problems they need to sort out. It also shows us that there are special places and rules to help everyone deal with these problems fairly. Let's keep cheering for Ghana football and remember to always be kind, respectful, and fair to everyone around us. Play nicely, share, and always tell the truth! That's how we make our communities strong and happy. **Did you know?** Ghana is one of the most famous football nations in Africa! We have played in the World Cup many times, showing the world how amazing African football is!

Always Be Kind

Remember, kids, it's always good to be kind, listen to others, and understand that sometimes people have big problems. The most important thing is that we all try our best to be fair and helpful.
summary: Our famous Ghanaian footballer, Partey, will go to a special place called court in the United Kingdom next month to talk about a serious problem from a few years ago and explain his side of the story.
whyMatters: This is important because everyone, even famous people like Partey, must follow rules and be fair, and courts help make sure everyone is heard and treated justly when big problems happen.

originalTitle: Cartoonist freed after 15 years in prison without charge in Eritrea
title: Cartoonist freed after 15 years in prison without charge in Eritrea
content: Biniam Solomon was known for his witty and critical cartoons before the free press was closed.

originalTitle: Bolivian authorities capture drug kingpin Sebastian Marset in police raid
title: A Big Bad Guy is Caught! Good News for Safety!
content:
Hello, Super Star Listeners!

Hello, hello, my amazing friends across Africa! This is your friendly news reporter, here to tell you something very important. We have some super good news today! It's all about making our world a safer place for everyone. We want all children, like you, to be safe and happy.

Big News from Far Away!

Guess what? Far, far away, in a country called Bolivia, something big happened! The brave police and special helpers caught a very, very bad person. This person was called Sebastian Marset. He was a very bad leader of bad people. This is big news because he did bad things that hurt people.

Who Was This Bad Guy?

Sebastian Marset was what we call a "drug kingpin." That sounds like a big word, doesn't it? Let me tell you what it means. A drug kingpin is a person who sells dangerous things called "drugs." These drugs are NOT medicine that helps you when you are sick. No, no! These drugs are very, very bad. They make people very sick. They make people sad. They can even make people lose their way and not think clearly. The drug kingpin wants to make lots of money from these bad drugs. They do not care if people get hurt. They only care about getting rich. That is why catching them is so important! Did you know? There are good medicines that doctors give you to help you get better when you are sick. But bad drugs are very different! They are like poison for your body and mind.

Where is Bolivia?

Bolivia is a beautiful country. It is very far from us here in Africa. It is in a place called South America. Imagine looking at a big map of the world. You would see Africa, where we are. Then, you would see big oceans. Across one big ocean, you would find South America. Bolivia is there! Just like in Africa, people in Bolivia want to live in peace and safety. That's why their police are so important. Did you know? The world has many different countries! Every country has its own police and leaders who work hard to keep people safe.

How Did the Police Catch Him?

Our brave police in Bolivia did something called a "police raid." What is a police raid? It means the police moved very quickly and quietly. They surprised the bad guys! They went to the place where Sebastian Marset was hiding. They had a very clever plan. The police worked together like a super team. They were very brave. And guess what? They caught him! They caught Sebastian Marset! This is a big win for good people everywhere. The police are like superheroes, protecting us every day. They try their best to catch all the bad people. Did you know? Police officers train very hard to be strong and smart. They learn how to solve puzzles and find clues to catch bad guys! They wear special uniforms and sometimes use special cars or tools to do their job.

Working Together: Bolivia and the USA!

After the police caught Sebastian Marset, he was put into "custody." This means he was taken to a safe place by the police. He was not free to go anywhere. He had to stay with the police. But the story doesn't end there! Sebastian Marset was then sent to another big country. This country is called the United States of America, or the USA. It is also very far away! The USA also wants to stop bad people who sell drugs. So, Bolivia and the USA worked together. They are like two good friends helping each other. The USA wants to stop drug sellers very strongly. They want to use "more aggressive antidrug tactics." This means they want to be even smarter and faster. They want to have very good plans to catch all drug sellers. They want to stop these bad drugs from hurting anyone else. So, Sebastian Marset is now with the USA police. They will make sure he cannot hurt anyone anymore. Working together makes us all stronger against bad things. Did you know? Countries often work together, like good neighbours, to solve big problems. It's called international cooperation! This helps make the whole world a safer place for everyone.

Why This is Good News for Everyone!

Catching bad guys like Sebastian Marset is very, very good news. Why? Because it helps to make our world safer! When people sell bad drugs, they hurt many families. They make towns and cities not safe. When a drug kingpin is caught, it means fewer bad drugs will be sold. It means fewer people will get hurt. It means more children, like you, can grow up in a safer and happier world. The police work so hard every day to keep us all safe. We should be very thankful for them. They are our heroes! Every time a bad guy is caught, it's a victory for good. Did you know? When communities are safe, children can play outside more, go to school happily, and learn many new things without worry!

What Can YOU Do to Help?

Even though you are still young, you can help make the world a better place! You can always be kind to your friends and family. You can listen to your parents and teachers. They always want to keep you safe. If you ever see something that looks strange or bad, you must tell a grown-up you trust. They will know what to do. Never take anything from strangers. Never go with someone you don't know. Your safety is very important! Be smart, be kind, and be brave! Did you know? Your voice is important! Always tell a grown-up if you feel unsafe or see something wrong. They are there to protect you.

Keep Our World Safe!

So, that's our big news story for today! A very bad person was caught. This means our world is a little bit safer. We are thankful for the brave police and for countries working together. Let's all try to be good citizens. Let's help each other. Let's make our beautiful world a safe and happy place for every child. Until next time, stay happy and stay safe!
summary: Police in Bolivia caught a very bad person who sold dangerous drugs, and now he will face justice to keep everyone safe.
whyMatters: This is important because catching bad people who sell dangerous drugs helps keep children and families safe and healthy.

originalTitle: Tottenham players can either 'cry or fight' - Tudor
title: Football Fun: Tottenham's Big Challenge!
content:
Jambo, Amazing Football Friends!

Hello, hello, everyone! It’s your kid-friendly news reporter from Africa, bringing you exciting news from the wonderful world of football! You know how much we love kicking a ball around, don’t you? Today, we have a story about a very famous football team far away in England, called Tottenham Hotspur.

Meet Tottenham Hotspur!

Imagine a group of super good football players. They wear white and blue clothes when they play. This team is called Tottenham Hotspur! Sometimes people just call them "Spurs." They are from a big city called London, in a country named England. They play in a very, very big stadium with many, many fans cheering for them. Just like you cheer for your favourite local team! Did you know? Football is the most popular sport in the whole world! Many children in Africa love to play football, just like the children in England!

What is the Premier League?

Now, imagine the biggest, most exciting football party in England. All the best teams get to come to this party and play games against each other. This big party is called the "Premier League." It's where all the strongest and fastest teams play. Everyone wants their team to be in the Premier League because it means they are one of the very best! Our team, Tottenham Hotspur, usually plays in this super important Premier League. They play against other amazing teams, trying to win as many games as they can. Every game is a big adventure!

Oh No, What is Relegation?

But sometimes, things can get a bit tricky in football. Imagine if a team loses many, many games in the Premier League. It's like not doing very well in a big school contest. If they lose too many, they might have to leave the big Premier League party. This is called "relegation." It means they would go down to a slightly smaller football league next year. It's like moving from the big kids' playground to a smaller one for a little while. No team wants to be relegated because they want to stay with the best of the best! Tottenham Hotspur is now trying very hard not to be relegated. Did you know? A normal football match lasts for 90 minutes! That's a lot of running and kicking!

Coach Igor Tudor's Big Words!

So, our friends at Tottenham Hotspur are in a difficult spot right now. They need to win games to stay in the Premier League. Their coach, the person who leads the team and tells them what to do, is a very clever man named Igor Tudor. He is like the teacher or the captain of the team. Coach Tudor said something very important to his players. He told them they can either "cry or fight." What do you think he means by that? It sounds like a big choice, doesn't it?

"Cry or Fight"? What Does It Mean?

Let's think about what "cry" means. If you cry, it usually means you are sad, or you feel like giving up. Maybe you lost a game, and you feel very down. If the players just cried, it would mean they are sad, and they stop trying their best. But then there is "fight"! When you fight (not with fists, but with your heart and strength!), it means you are brave. It means you don't give up, even when things are tough. It means you work super hard, you run fast, you kick the ball strong, and you help your teammates. It means you keep trying and trying, no matter what! Coach Tudor wants his team to be strong and brave. He wants them to choose to "fight" on the football field! Did you know? There are 11 players on a football team when they play a match!

Why Fighting is Best!

Choosing to "fight" is always the best choice, isn't it? When you are playing a game with your friends, or learning something new at school, if it gets hard, you don't just cry and give up. No! You try harder! Tottenham Hotspur's players need to work together like a strong family. They need to pass the ball, talk to each other, and defend their goal. They need to run with all their energy until the final whistle blows. They need to be brave and show courage! This is called teamwork and resilience. It means being strong inside and working with your friends to reach a goal. Just like when you and your friends play together to win a game of football in your village!

Football is Fun for Everyone!

So, even though Tottenham Hotspur is facing a big challenge, we know they will try their very best. Because that's what good football teams do! They play with all their heart, for their fans, and for the love of the game. Whether it's a famous team in England or a small team playing on a dusty field in Africa, the spirit of football is the same: try hard, work together, and never give up! It's a game that brings joy and excitement to everyone!

Let's Cheer for All Teams!

So, let's cheer for Tottenham Hotspur to be strong and brave! And let's remember that in life, just like in football, it's always better to fight for what you want and never, ever give up. Go, go, go, everyone! This is your reporter, signing off with a big smile! Kwaheri for now!
summary: A famous football team called Tottenham Hotspur is in a tough spot and needs to play very hard to stay in the top league, and their coach wants them to be brave and fight, not give up, just like we should always try our best.
whyMatters: This story matters because it teaches us that even famous teams face big challenges, and it's important to always try your best, be brave, and work together, no matter how difficult things get, just like we do in school or playing games.
